title: Percent, Rates, Markups, Discounts, and Repeated Change · Decision Notebook: Percent Claims
summary: Audit prices, discounts, taxes, tips, population changes, and performance claims. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg percent claims increment in a consumer percent-claim audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Detect denominator switching, percentage-point confusion, and selective baselines in public claims.
Model repeated percent change with compounding rather than repeated addition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Percent Claims evidence
steps.instruction: A price of $100 is reduced by 14%. What is the new price?
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use 100 × (1 − 14/100), then compare the result with the original price.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use 100 × (1 − 14/100), then compare the result with the original price.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.

steps.title: Defend the Percent Claims model
